title: Midday Review — Wednesday, July 9, 2026
html:
Midday Review — Wednesday, July 9, 2026

*Written for a 3-minute read on your phone. If you read nothing else today, read sections 1 and 2.*

1. NEED FROM YOU

1. Approve the website update when I hand it to you (coming today). The fresh player ratings are ready to show on the public site, and publishing changes what visitors see, so it needs your click. I'll bring it with a one-click undo already in hand.

2. WHAT GOT BETTER TODAY

Player ratings were 10 months out of date — now fixed. They'd been frozen since last September, and 116 of 202 current players had no rating at all. Now every player is rated, and we added an alarm that shouts loudly if ratings ever go stale again.

We answered the big worry — were stale ratings hiding real accuracy? Mostly no. With fresh ratings the model got a little more accurate (average error about 10.9 down to about 10.6 points, on the 143 games we can compare head-to-head; across all decided games the figure runs a bit higher, near 11.1 — same model, different slice). Single-game margins are near the luck floor for everyone, us and Vegas alike.

Total-points betting ranges were too narrow — now correctly sized. Our predicted range for a game's combined score was tighter than reality warranted, so it's been widened to match.

The database was grinding overnight — now fast. The worst offender went from reading hundreds of gigabytes a night down to a few megabytes.

3. WHAT WE LEARNED

Predicting a single game's winner and margin is mostly luck — for everyone, including Vegas. So we stop chasing raw margin accuracy and instead hunt specific spots where the book's number looks wrong. We're building the map for that hunt right now, and the first version lands today.

We tested four new information sources today — none helped yet, because we're missing the data. Injury-report wording, day-of availability swings, lineup-news freshness, and travel/jet-lag all came back flat. Mostly that's because we don't yet collect timestamped lineup news or reliable travel data — which tells us exactly what to go get.

4. WHAT'S RUNNING NOW

Building the error map. Charting where we're least accurate and where we beat Vegas. A win means a clear shortlist of profitable spots to bet.

Rolling the fresh ratings onto the live site and switching on the freshness alarm. A win means the public numbers are current and can never silently go stale again.

Independently double-checking the fresh-ratings rollout. A win means it's confirmed correct before it goes public.

5. ALREADY HANDLED

  • Stale, frozen player ratings — fixed (the big win above).
  • The odds-data key that was in old code — reviewed and closed: the repository is private and the current code is scrubbed, so no rotation needed.
  • Total-points ranges — recalibrated to the right width.
  • Database running slow overnight — fixed.

6. WATCH LIST

  • The live public website still carries a database key inside its page code — a real public exposure worth closing by moving it off the page. Last checked July 9.
  • The automatic nightly ratings-refresh isn't switched on yet — the fresh ratings are already live, but the hands-off nightly rebuild still needs one build step; the freshness alarm catches any drift until then. Last checked July 9.
  • We're missing several data feeds — timestamped lineup news, reliable travel data, and backfilled depth charts — that block the next accuracy gains. The acquisition list has started. Last checked July 9.
